preamble: The debuccalization of the voiced labiodental fricative [v] into the glottal fricative [h] is a documented form of lenition, typically occurring through intermediate bilabial or approximant stages in specific phonetic environments.

examples.note: Alternation in specific suffixes and phonetic environments where the voiced fricative weakens to a glottal fricative.
Extreme lenition in colloquial dialects where voiced fricatives/approximants are debuccalized before potential elision.

sources: Phonological studies on Arabic and Romance lenition.
